fix(loop): persist commit_nudged across TUI run() invocations

The TUI calls loop::run() once per user turn, so a local
`commit_nudged` variable reset to false every message — causing
the "you made file changes" nudge to fire on every turn even
after a successful commit.

Move commit_nudged onto IntentDocument (which persists for the
entire session lifetime). Also reset it when the commit tool
clears files_modified, so a second commit after further changes
still gets a nudge.

Fixes: commit nudge regression introduced in rc.7"

feat(instruments-test): In core/crates/omegon/src/tui/instruments.rs, add a #[cfg(test)] mod with a test named `set_cleave_progress_replaces_snapshot`. Create an InstrumentPanel, call set_cleave_progress with a CleaveProgress (active=true, run_id='r1', total_children=2, completed=0, failed=0, children=vec![], total_tokens_in=0, total_tokens_out=0), assert cleave_progress is Some and run_id=='r1', then call set_cleave_progress again with run_id='r2' and assert the new run_id=='r2'. Run cargo test -p omegon to confirm it passes.

feat(retry-test): In core/crates/omegon/src/loop.rs, find the retry backoff constants (RETRY_BASE_MS, RETRY_MAX_MS or similar) and the delay formula. Add a #[cfg(test)] test named `retry_backoff_is_capped` that reconstructs the backoff formula inline and asserts that for attempt indices 0, 1, 2, 10, and 100 the computed delay never exceeds the cap constant. Run cargo test -p omegon to confirm it passes.

feat(tokens-test): In core/crates/omegon/src/features/cleave.rs, add a test named `apply_progress_event_accumulates_tokens` inside the existing #[cfg(test)] mod. It should: create a CleaveProgress with one child (label='alpha', zero token counts), call apply_progress_event with ProgressEvent::ChildTokens { child: 'alpha'.into(), tokens_in: 100, tokens_out: 50 }, then assert child.tokens_in==100, child.tokens_out==50, progress.total_tokens_in==100, progress.total_tokens_out==50. Run cargo test -p omegon to confirm it passes.
